data.content:
Your project uses .NET 10 with Microsoft.Testing.Platform. You can use `dotnet watch` to automatically re-run tests on every file save:

```
dotnet watch test --filter "RemoveStock_ExceedsAvailable_ThrowsException"
```

Let me start it for you so you can see it in action:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet watch test with filter

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet watch test with filter

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to set up hot reload for their test project. Since they're using .NET 10 with Microsoft.Testing.Platform (as configured in global.json), they can use `dotnet test --hotreload` or `dotnet watch test` to get hot reload for tests.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. With .NET and Microsoft.Testing.Platform, the best way to do hot reload for tests is `dotnet watch test`. But there's also `dotnet test --hotreload` which was introduced more recently.

Actually, for .NET 10 with Microsoft.Testing.Platform, the recommended approach is `dotnet test --hotreload` which watches for file changes and re-runs tests automatically.


I notice there's a bug in the `RemoveStock` method where it doesn't validate that the quantity doesn't exceed available stock, allowing negative values, but that's separate from what the user is asking about. For the hot reload setup, I should recommend `dotnet watch test` as the traditional approach, though `dotnet test --hotreload` might also work with .NET 10 and Microsoft.Testing.Platform.

arguments.description: Run dotnet watch test with filter

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-d1fcfa0dc1e84efcaa0a59169ecfee72 && dotnet watch test --filter \"RemoveStock_ExceedsAvailable_ThrowsException\" 2>&1 | head -40", "description": "Run dotnet watch test with filter", "initial_wait": 30}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet watch test with filter

data.reasoningText:
The issue is that the project needs to opt into Microsoft.Testing.Platform explicitly. The global.json says to use Microsoft.Testing.Platform, but the project doesn't have the right properties set. I need to add `<UseMicrosoftTestingPlatformRunner>true</UseMicrosoftTestingPlatformRunner>` or `<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>` to the csproj.

Actually, looking at the error message more carefully: "global.json defines test runner to be Microsoft.Testing.Platform. All projects must use that test runner." and "TestProject.csproj" is listed as using VSTest.


For MSTest 3.8.0, I need to enable the MSTest runner and set the output type to executable in the project file.

Updating the csproj...
